content.full_text:
F P P C DOBBS & NIELSEN
Attorneys and Counselors at Law
MAY 5 1246PM 1650, THE ALCOA BUILDING
ONE MARITIME PLAZA
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111

TELEPHONE (415) 362-1940


April 28, 1978


Ms. Regina Kutchins
Para Political Practices Commission
Sacramento, CA

Dear Ms. Kutchins:

     This office inquired today regarding the applicability of
FPPC Regulation-18116, adopted by the commission on 12-8-77,
relating to filing deadlines which fall on a Saturday, Sunday
or official state holiday.

     Although the language of the regulation refers to all
statements or reports, the regulation makes reference to
Government Code §81005, which deals only with Chapter 4 Campaign Filings.

     We inquired whether the regulation also encompasses Government
Code §§81005.1 and 81005.2, respectively dealing with
Chapter 6 (Lobbying Reports) and Chapter 7 (Statement of Economic Interest Reports).

     We were advised by your staff that the regulation includes
all types of filings. We intend to rely upon this advice.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Peter A. Bagatelos
                                        Peter A. Bagatelos

PAB/mw

[illegible] Provided by Allan Herndon.
Concern with interpretation.
[illegible] S K


SACRAMENTO OFFICE • SUITE 900 1006 FOURTH STREET, SACRAMENTO, CA 95814 • (916) 443-2051

sections.question_synthetic: Does FPPC Regulation 18116 regarding filing deadline extensions for weekends and holidays apply to Chapter 6 (Lobbying Reports) and Chapter 7 (Statement of Economic Interest Reports) filings, or only to Chapter 4 (Campaign Filings)?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC staff advised that Regulation 18116 encompasses all types of filings, including Lobbying Reports and Statement of Economic Interest Reports.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence documenting an attorney's inquiry and FPPC staff's oral advice response, not a formal FPPC advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The document summarizes the inquiry and staff response rather than providing formal legal analysis. Classified as correspondence rather than formal advice_letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does FPPC Regulation 18116 regarding filing deadline extensions for weekends and holidays apply to Chapter 6 (Lobbying Reports) and Chapter 7 (Statement of Economic Interest Reports) filings, or only to Chapter 4 (Campaign Filings)?

CONCLUSION: FPPC staff advised that Regulation 18116 encompasses all types of filings, including Lobbying Reports and Statement of Economic Interest Reports.

Although the language of the regulation refers to all statements or reports, the regulation makes reference to Government Code §81005, which deals only with Chapter 4 Campaign Filings. We inquired whether the regulation also encompasses Government Code §§81005.1 and 81005.2, respectively dealing with Chapter 6 (Lobbying Reports) and Chapter 7 (Statement of Economic Interest Reports). We were advised by your staff that the regulation includes all types of filings. We intend to rely upon this advice. Sincerely, Peter A. Bagatelos Peter A. Bagatelos PAB/mw [illegible] Provided by Allan Herndon. Concern with interpretation. [illegible] S K SACRAMENTO OFFICE • SUITE 900 1006 FOURTH STREET, SACRAMENTO, CA 95814 • (916) 443-2051
embedding.summary: This is correspondence from an attorney documenting an inquiry made to FPPC staff regarding the scope of Regulation 18116 concerning filing deadline extensions and confirming that the regulation applies to all filing types under Government Code sections 81005, 81005.1, and 81005.2.
